attributes.action: Ban beehives in public spaces & require safety signage to prevent attacks.
attributes.additional_details:
Ban beehives in public‑access areas where people, cannot avoid them.Require clear, visible safety signage whenever beekeepers are working on or inspecting hives.Set national safety standards for hive placement near communities.Introduce enforcement & accountability measures to protect the public.
These changes are needed to prevent further incidents like the one where my daughter & grandson suffered over 150 stings each, were airlifted to hospital, & continue to experience trauma.
attributes.background: My daughter and grandson suffered over 150 bee stings each in a public area with no warning signs and had to be airlifted to hospital. I want the UK Government to ban beehives in public spaces and require safety signage when beekeepers are working.

departments.name: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

rejection.details:
Your petition is about something that the Scottish Government and Scottish Parliament is responsible for. That means that the UK Government and Parliament can't look into it. 

The Scottish Parliament has its own Public Petitions Committee. If you'd like to send your petition to the Scottish Parliament, you can find out more here: https://petitions.parliament.scot/

You may also wish to contact your local council and MSP who represent you about this issue. 

You can find the name of your local council and how to contact them here: https://www.mygov.scot/find-your-local-council

You can find the name of your local MSP and how to contact them here: https://www.parliament.scot/msps
